# Task Icon Convention

**Purpose:** Every `TaskCreate` call MUST use **ICON + [Role]** format in `subject` and `activeForm`. This displays agent identity in the spinner вҖ” a key UX signal for end users watching task progress.

---

## Format

```
subject:    "ICON [Role] Brief description"
activeForm: "ICON Verb-ing description"
```

## Icon Map

| Agent Type | Icon | Example subject |
|------------|------|----------------|
| Explore | рҹ”Қ | `рҹ”Қ [Explorer] Find entity files` |
| coder / sparc-coder | рҹ’» | `рҹ’» [Coder] Implement auth service` |
| tester | рҹ§Ә | `рҹ§Ә [Tester] Run unit test suite` |
| reviewer | рҹ“Ӣ | `рҹ“Ӣ [Reviewer] Review PR changes` |
| researcher | рҹ”¬ | `рҹ”¬ [Researcher] Analyze dependencies` |
| planner / Plan | рҹ“җ | `рҹ“җ [Planner] Design migration plan` |
| security-architect / security-* | рҹӣЎпёҸ | `рҹӣЎпёҸ [Security] Audit auth middleware` |
| architect / system-architect | рҹҸ—пёҸ | `рҹҸ—пёҸ [Architect] Design API structure` |
| backend-dev | вҡҷпёҸ | `вҡҷпёҸ [Backend] Build REST endpoints` |
| mobile-dev | рҹ“ұ | `рҹ“ұ [Mobile] Fix React Native layout` |
| performance-* | вҡЎ | `вҡЎ [Perf] Profile query bottleneck` |
| general-purpose | рҹӨ– | `рҹӨ– [Agent] Execute multi-step task` |

## Examples

**Wrong:** `subject: "Run infrastructure tests"` (no icon, no role)

**Right:** `subject: "рҹ§Ә [Tester] Run infrastructure tests"` (icon + role prefix)

## Why This Matters

The spinner is the primary visual feedback during agent execution. Without icons, all tasks look identical вҖ” a wall of plain text. With icons, users can instantly identify:
- What type of agent is working
- Whether the right specialist was chosen
- When agent types change during multi-step workflows
